1.Installing Packages required for the task and creating working directory

#As libraries are already installed
# seting working directory
setwd("C:/Users/abrup/OneDrive/Desktop//LSI-2/Data_Vis_final_project")

2.Libraries

#loading libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggtext)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)
library(ggrepel)

3.TCGA expression data

3.1 Load and prepare data

#loading required data
tcga_cancer_csv <- read.csv("C:/Users/abrup/OneDrive/Desktop/LSI-2/Data_Vis_final_project/Data_Vis_R/tcga_cancer.csv")
tcga_cancer_labels_csv <- read.csv("C:/Users/abrup/OneDrive/Desktop/LSI-2/Data_Vis_final_project/Data_Vis_R/tcga_cancer_labels.csv")
#preparing data
tcga_samples <- tcga_cancer_csv[0:200, 3:45]#select 200 samples for gene 1 to gene 43
tcga_samples_label <- tcga_cancer_labels_csv[1:200,]#select samples from cancer label 

#Check the first few rows and columns  of the selected tcga_samples and tcga_samples_labels to check data 
head(tcga_samples[,1:6]) 
##      gene_1   gene_2   gene_3    gene_4 gene_5   gene_6
## 1 2.0172093 3.265527 5.478487 10.431999      0 7.175175
## 2 0.5927321 1.588421 7.586157  9.623011      0 6.816049
## 3 3.5117590 4.327199 6.881787  9.870730      0 6.972130
## 4 3.6636179 4.507649 6.659068 10.196184      0 7.843375
## 5 2.6557411 2.821547 6.539454  9.738265      0 6.566967
## 6 3.4678533 3.581918 6.620243  9.706829      0 7.758510
head(tcga_samples_label)
##          X Class
## 1 sample_0  PRAD
## 2 sample_1  LUAD
## 3 sample_2  PRAD
## 4 sample_3  PRAD
## 5 sample_4  BRCA
## 6 sample_5  PRAD

3.2 Plotting

3.2.1 Expression distribution Gene1

gene1<-tcga_samples[,1]#select gene1 from tcga_samples
histo1<-ggplot(tcga_samples, aes(x = gene1)) + 
  #choose binwidth of 0.4
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.4, color = "black", fill = "#cccccc", linewidth  = 0.7) + 
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 45), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    labs(title = "Expression Gene 1", x = "RNA counts", y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Customize the theme
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"), 
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
      axis.ticks = element_line(color = "#5A5A5A", linewidth = 1.5),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(.15, "cm"),
      panel.grid = element_blank(), # No grid
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "#5A5A5A", fill = NA, size = 2)
    )

print(histo1)

Bin width in a histogram is a value which divide the given variable into equal parts and it determine how much bar should be displayed in the plot and also it depends on the characteristics of our data set and the insight which we want to derive from it .

Below are two histogram with two different bin width .one higher(0.6) and lower (0.3) .If the data set is scattered we can go for higher bin width but it will display unwanted information

If bin width is small it will provide more details which include small fluctuations

So here bin width of 0.4 is the best choice were it can able to provide a both detail and clarity

#Histogram with higher bin width
histo2<-ggplot(tcga_samples, aes(x = gene1)) + 
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.6, color = "black", fill = "#cccccc", linewidth  = 0.7) + 
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 70), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    labs(title = "Expression Gene 1", x = "RNA counts", y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Customize the theme
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"), 
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
      axis.ticks = element_line(color = "#5A5A5A", linewidth = 1.5),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(.15, "cm"),
      panel.grid = element_blank(), # No grid
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "#5A5A5A", fill = NA, size = 2)
    )

print(histo2)

```

#Histogram with lower bin width
histo3<-ggplot(tcga_samples, aes(x = gene1)) + 
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.3, color = "black", fill = "#cccccc", linewidth  = 0.7) + 
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 70), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    labs(title = "Expression Gene 1", x = "RNA counts", y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Customize the theme
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"), 
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
      axis.ticks = element_line(color = "#5A5A5A", linewidth = 1.5),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(.15, "cm"),
      panel.grid = element_blank(), # No grid
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "#5A5A5A", fill = NA, size = 2)
    )

print(histo3)

3.2.2 Gene expression correlation

#extracting gene 1 and gene 12 from dataset
gene_data <- data.frame(gene1 = tcga_samples[, 1], gene12 = tcga_samples[, 12])
# Calculate Pearson correlation (R)
correlation <- cor(gene_data$gene1, gene_data$gene12, method = "pearson")
# Create the scatter plot
scatter_plot <- ggplot(data = gene_data, aes(x = gene1, y = gene12)) +
  geom_point(
    color = "black",
    fill = "lightblue",
    shape = 21,
    size = 3.6,
    stroke = 0.50
  ) +
  #Pearson correlation coefficient (R)
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = paste("R =", round(correlation, 3)), 
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = 2, size = 5, color = "black") + 
  #add linear line to the plot
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm', color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, se = TRUE) + 
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Gene 1 and Gene 12",
    x = "Gene 1",
    y = "Gene 12"
  ) +
  #customized theme
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "#5A5A5A", linewidth = 1.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "lightgrey",linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "lightgrey",linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "#5A5A5A", fill = NA, size = 2),
  )


scatter_plot

Correlation tells how closely two variables are related and whether changes in one variable are associated with changes in the other which is denoted by letter R and it ranges from -1 to 1 . 1 indicate a positive correlation and -1 indicate negative correlation where 0 means no relation

In the given diagram R=-0.62 which is very close to 0 which suggest that there almost no relation between gene 1 and gene 12 .even though there is a slight tendency which is indicated by negative value but it is weak and negligible

The gray area represent the prediction interval it tell about the true regression line .A wider gray line indicate more uncertainty and lower indicate precision. In the given diagram it is wide due to weak correlation and there is lot of uncertainty between gene 1 and gene 12 .which means the data is not giving enough relationship between both Genes.

3.2.3 Class proportions

#create a data frame from tcga samples
barchart_data <- tcga_samples_label %>%
  mutate(Class = factor(Class, levels = c("BRCA", "COAD", "KIRC", "LUAD", "PRAD"))) %>%
  group_by(Class) %>%
  #calculate  absolute  frequency for each cancer labels
  summarise(abs_freq = n()) %>%
  # Calculate the percentage of each cancer type and add two column
  mutate(Proportion = abs_freq / 2,
         pos = 1) #finding the proportions from the samples 
head(barchart_data)
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   Class abs_freq Proportion   pos
##   <fct>    <int>      <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 BRCA        73       36.5     1
## 2 COAD        14        7       1
## 3 KIRC        34       17       1
## 4 LUAD        40       20       1
## 5 PRAD        39       19.5     1
#creating  the Stacked bar charts 
propo_plot  <- ggplot(barchart_data, aes(x = pos, y = Proportion, fill = Class)) +
  geom_bar(color = "#636363", linewidth = 1, stat = "identity", width = 0.5) + # use the values already provided (Proportion)
  geom_text(aes(label = abs_freq), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 4) + 
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Classes (n = 200)",
    y = "Percentage (%)",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
     # Adjust plot margins
    axis.ticks =  element_line(color = "#5A5A5A",linewidth = 1.5),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(.15, "cm"),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),  
    plot.margin = unit(c(1.5, 15, 1.5, 5.5), "points"), 
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "#5A5A5A", fill = NA, size = 2),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12), 
    legend.position = "top",  
  ) +
 
  coord_flip() +  # Flip coordinates for  horizontal bars
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0.75, 1.25)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +  
  theme(aspect.ratio = .1) +
  #Use a custom color palette for the bar chart
  scale_fill_discrete(breaks = c("PRAD","LUAD", "KIRC", "COAD", "BRCA"),
                      #pastel 1 reversed order
                      type = rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(5, "Pastel1"))) 

propo_plot

The color which chosen is a good choice for visualizing the distribution of classes.It helps in

differentiating between each class. Even though I think using a more varied color palette with

higher contrast and are color blind friendly like “cbPalette” would be a better color choice than this

3.2.4 Combine the plots

# Combine gene expression histogram plot and correlation plot side-by-side in the upper panel
upper <- (histo1 | scatter_plot) + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2)) 

# Combine the upper panel with propo_plot in a vertical stack
final_plot <- (upper / propo_plot) + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(2, 1)) + # Adjust height ratio
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'I') # Add tags 


final_plot

4 Human genome

#loading data
hg38_gene <- readr::read_tsv("C:/Users/abrup/OneDrive/Desktop/LSI-2/Data_Vis_final_project//Data_Vis_R/hg38_genes.tsv")
hg38_gene <- as.data.frame(hg38_gene)
head(hg38_gene)
##     chr start_gene  end_gene strand_gene       gene_name      ensembl_id
## 1 chr19   35414881  35415149           -       RN7SL491P ENSG00000264400
## 2 chr15   27621534  27629289           + ENSG00000258594 ENSG00000258594
## 3  chr2   95477008  95484754           -         TRIM43B ENSG00000144010
## 4 chr15   96354237  96405235           + ENSG00000259275 ENSG00000259275
## 5 chr16   27313668  27364778           +            IL4R ENSG00000077238
## 6  chr6  128928995 128929988           -          MESTP1 ENSG00000220884
##              gene_type
## 1             misc_RNA
## 2               lncRNA
## 3       protein_coding
## 4               lncRNA
## 5       protein_coding
## 6 processed_pseudogene
#filtering data based on gene type
hg38_fil<- filter(hg38_gene, gene_type %in% c("protein_coding", "lncRNA", "processed_pseudogene", "transcribed_unprocessed_pseudogene"))
head(hg38_fil)
##     chr start_gene  end_gene strand_gene       gene_name      ensembl_id
## 1 chr15   27621534  27629289           + ENSG00000258594 ENSG00000258594
## 2  chr2   95477008  95484754           -         TRIM43B ENSG00000144010
## 3 chr15   96354237  96405235           + ENSG00000259275 ENSG00000259275
## 4 chr16   27313668  27364778           +            IL4R ENSG00000077238
## 5  chr6  128928995 128929988           -          MESTP1 ENSG00000220884
## 6  chr1   22028317  22052927           -       CDC42-AS1 ENSG00000285752
##              gene_type
## 1               lncRNA
## 2       protein_coding
## 3               lncRNA
## 4       protein_coding
## 5 processed_pseudogene
## 6               lncRNA
# Rename and group the gene types
hg38_fil <- hg38_fil %>%
  mutate(
    gene_type = case_when(
      gene_type == "protein_coding" ~ "mRNA",
      gene_type %in% c("processed_pseudogene", "transcribed_unprocessed_pseudogene") ~ "Pseudo",
      TRUE ~ gene_type
    )
  )
# Calculate gene length and filter
hg38<- hg38_fil %>%
  mutate(gene_length =  end_gene - start_gene) %>%
  filter(gene_length > 1000 & gene_length <= 20000)

# View the final dataframe
head(hg38)
##     chr start_gene  end_gene strand_gene       gene_name      ensembl_id
## 1 chr15   27621534  27629289           + ENSG00000258594 ENSG00000258594
## 2  chr2   95477008  95484754           -         TRIM43B ENSG00000144010
## 3 chr19   15571762  15573650           + ENSG00000290595 ENSG00000290595
## 4  chr1  157925065 157929862           + ENSG00000236957 ENSG00000236957
## 5  chr5  180826871 180831621           -            HEIH ENSG00000278970
## 6  chrY    2609415   2623453           +          CD99P1 ENSG00000292361
##   gene_type gene_length
## 1    lncRNA        7755
## 2      mRNA        7746
## 3    lncRNA        1888
## 4    Pseudo        4797
## 5    lncRNA        4750
## 6    Pseudo       14038

4.2 Plotting

4.2.1 Gene count

gene_plot <- ggplot(hg38,aes(gene_type))+
  labs(title = "Total gene number" , x = "Class", y = "Count")+
  theme_minimal() +
  #custommize theme
  theme(
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.15, "cm"),  
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, color = "black", angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "#636363", linewidth = 0.75),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),  
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "lightgrey", linewidth = 1, linetype = "dashed"),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "lightgrey", fill = NA, size = 1)
  ) +
  geom_bar(aes(fill = gene_type), color= "#636363", linewidth= 1,  width = 0.75,
           show.legend = F, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0, 4000))+
  scale_fill_discrete(type = brewer.pal(3, "Dark2"))+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Pseudo", "mRNA", "lncRNA"))
print(gene_plot)

4.2.2 Length distribution

# Calculate median gene length for each gene_type
hg38_sel <- hg38 %>%
  mutate(gene_length = end_gene - start_gene)

median_gene_lengths <- hg38_sel%>%
  mutate(gene_length = end_gene - start_gene) %>% # Add gene_length column
  group_by(gene_type) %>%                     
  summarise(median_length = median(gene_length)) # Calculate median

print(median_gene_lengths)
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   gene_type median_length
##   <chr>             <dbl>
## 1 Pseudo             1547
## 2 lncRNA             4438
## 3 mRNA               8251
# Extract the median values for each gene type
me_pseudo <- median_gene_lengths$median_length[median_gene_lengths$gene_type == "Pseudo"]
me_mRNA <- median_gene_lengths$median_length[median_gene_lengths$gene_type == "mRNA"]
me_lncRNA <- median_gene_lengths$median_length[median_gene_lengths$gene_type == "lncRNA"]

# Create a density plot to visualize the gene length distribution for each gene type
density_plot<- ggplot(hg38_sel, aes(x=gene_length, fill = fct_rev(gene_type))) +
  #add vertical line based on each gene's type 
  geom_vline(data = median_gene_lengths, aes(xintercept = me_pseudo, color = "#D95F02"), 
             linewidth = 0.7, show.legend = F) +  
  geom_vline(data = median_gene_lengths, aes(xintercept = me_mRNA, color = "#1B9E77"), 
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7, show.legend = F) +  
  geom_vline(data = median_gene_lengths, aes(xintercept = me_lncRNA, color = "#7570B3"), 
             linetype = "longdash", linewidth = 0.7, show.legend = F) +  
  geom_density(color = "black", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.7) +  
  #manuely fill colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pseudo" = "#7570B3", "mRNA" = "#D95F02", "lncRNA" = "#1B9E77"))+
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0))+
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(1000,20500))+
  labs(title = "Length distribution of gene classes",
       x = "Gene Length (bp)", y = "Density") +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 13, color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.x =  element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "lightgrey",linewidth = 0.7),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 17),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "lightgrey" ,linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y =  element_line(color = "lightgrey" ,linewidth = 0.5),
    legend.position= c(0.82, 0.85),
    legend.title=element_blank(),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill='transparent'),
    legend.box.background = element_blank(),
  )

# Display the plot
print(density_plot)

An alternative approach for overlapping density plot is Box plot and Violin Plot . In box plot it show both median ,spread of data and also any outliers. Violin plot is also a better option which shows the information like box plot and in addition it shows how densely the data points are packed together for different lengths.

Below I have drawn both box and violin plot

#box plot 
box_plot<- ggplot(hg38_sel, aes(x=gene_type, y=gene_length, fill = gene_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 2, outlier.colour = "red", alpha = 0.7) +  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pseudo" = "#7570B3", "mRNA" = "#D95F02", "lncRNA" = "#1B9E77")) +  
  labs(title = "Length distribution of gene classes",
       x = "Gene Type", y = "Gene Length (bp)") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "lightgrey" ,linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y =  element_line(color = "lightgrey" ,linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 17),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 13, color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.x =  element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "lightgrey",linewidth = 0.7),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "lightgrey", fill = NA, size = 1),
  )

# Display the boxplot
print(box_plot)

#violin plot
violin_plot <- ggplot(hg38_sel, aes(x=gene_type, y=gene_length, fill = gene_type)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.7) +   
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pseudo" = "#7570B3", "mRNA" = "#D95F02", "lncRNA" = "#1B9E77")) +  
  labs(title = "Length distribution of gene classes",
       x = "Gene Type", y = "Gene Length (bp)") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.y =  element_line(color = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 17),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 13, color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.x =  element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.7),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "lightgrey", fill = NA, size = 1),
  )

# Display the violin plot
print(violin_plot)

4.2.3 Include figure

# Define the additional theme for adjusting the legend position
combined_plots <- density_plot + theme(legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, 'cm'))

# Combine the two plots side by side
combined_plot2 <- gene_plot + combined_plots + 
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 3)) +  # Adjust relative widths of the plots
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A')  # Add plot annotation tags 

# Display the combined plot
combined_plot2

5 Differntial gene Expression

5.1 load and prepare data

# Read the data
deseq_data <- read.delim("C:/Users/abrup/OneDrive/Desktop/LSI-2/Data_Vis_final_project/Data_Vis_R/deseq2_dummy.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
# Select the top 2000 genes according to the average of the normalized count values
top_2000_genes <- deseq_data %>%
  #sorting
  arrange(desc(baseMean)) %>%
  slice(1:2000) %>%
  #modify based on the given values
  mutate(
    significant = case_when(
      log2FoldChange > 0 & padj < 0.1 ~ "up",
      log2FoldChange < 0 & padj < 0.1 ~ "down",
      TRUE ~ "n.s"
    ),
    label = ifelse(padj < 0.1 & log2FoldChange > 2, gene_id, NA)
  )
# Create the volcano plot
volcano_plot <- ggplot(top_2000_genes, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = significant), shape = 21, size = 2, alpha = .75, stroke = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("up" = "brown", "down" = "skyblue", "n.s" = "white")) +
  labs(
    title = "Diff. expressed genes (Cancer vs. Normal)",
    x = "Fold change (log2)",
    y = "Adjusted p-value (-log10)",
    fill = "Regulation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_label_repel(
    aes(label = label),
    nudge_y = 0.5,
    nudge_x = 0.5,
    box.padding = 0.5,
    point.padding = 0.5,
    na.rm = TRUE,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  # adding vertical and horizontal line
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.5) +
  #Customizing the coordinate system
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2, 5), ylim = c(0.4, 10), expand = TRUE) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(linewidth = 2, fill = NA),
    legend.position = c(0.9, 0.82),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5),
    legend.key.width = unit(1.2, "cm")
  )

# Display the volcano plot
volcano_plot

5.2.2 Heatmaps

# Filter the top 20 genes based on adjusted p-value
deseq2_res_f <- deseq_data %>%
  slice_min(n = 20, order_by = padj)

# Create the plot
deseq2_res_f %>% 
  dplyr::select(gene_id, 9:14) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = 2:7, names_to = "condition", values_to = "counts") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = gene_id, fill = log2(counts), label = round(log2(counts), digits = 1))) +
  geom_tile(color = "black") +
  geom_text(color = "black") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.4, 20.6), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_distiller(breaks = c(2.5, 7.5, 12.5), palette = "Purples", direction = 1) +
  labs(x = "Sample", y = "Gene IDs", fill = "Counts (log2)") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top", 
    legend.ticks = element_blank(),
    title = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, colour = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    panel.border = element_rect(size = 3, fill = NA)
  )
